content:
---
title: "Cross-property pattern detection uses embedding similarity in sliding time windows"
description: "pgvector cosine similarity >0.85 across properties in 4-hour windows detects when 3+ properties ask the same question"
date: 2026-03-14
group: zk
type: note
id: "20260314-cross-property-pattern-detection-via-embeddings"
links:
  - "20260314-rag-over-fine-tuning-for-property-knowledge"
  - "20260314-two-scopes-one-pipeline"
  - "20260314-campaign-kb-corporate-property-bridge"
  - "20260314-corporate-property-communication-fault-line"
source: "projects/autoproxy/AI_ARCHITECTURE"
created_by: agent
---

Cross-property pattern detection is one of AutoProxy's most valuable corporate features. When multiple properties ask the same question within a short time window, it signals either a common confusion about a new initiative, a shared operational problem, or a policy gap. Detecting these patterns lets corporate respond once instead of N times.

The implementation is lightweight and database-native:

1. Every classified message already has an embedding (computed for RAG retrieval).
2. A sliding time window (default 4 hours) of message embeddings is maintained per-property in Postgres.
3. An Oban cron job runs every 15 minutes. For each message in the window, it finds messages from other properties with cosine similarity > 0.85 using pgvector.
4. Similar messages are clustered using single-linkage clustering (DBSCAN is unnecessary at this scale).
5. Clusters spanning 3+ properties trigger a "cross-property pattern" alert to HQ.
6. The alert includes a canonical question (LLM-generated summary of the cluster), which properties asked, and message excerpts.

The performance math works: at 200 properties sending 50 messages/day each, a 4-hour window contains approximately 2,000 messages. A similarity query with an IVFFlat index over 2,000 vectors takes under 50ms. This is Postgres-native -- no specialized vector database needed.

The resolution flow creates a virtuous cycle: corporate provides one answer to the detected pattern, which creates KnowledgeEntries at all affected properties, which means future occurrences of the same question are auto-resolved via the on-site RAG pipeline. Each pattern detection reduces future human workload permanently.

The PatternGroup resource tracks the lifecycle: detected -> LLM generates canonical question and consolidated answer -> regional manager reviews -> distributed as knowledge entry or direct response.
